pr.title: Improve matching in Melee source files
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Open broad matching PR touching 57 `src/melee` C files across effect, fighter, game, stage, interface, menu, library, map, and toy code. The PR body reports a saved-baseline result of matched code 70.39% (+0.24%, +9176 bytes) and matched data 41.33% (+0.06%, +712 bytes), with 13 new matches, 123 unmatched-item improvements, and no reported broken matches or unmatched regressions. Review requested changes: avoid pragma/fake-match shortcuts, convert repeated code to loops, replace pointer math/M2C artifacts with typed structs/fields, and recognize `jobj.h` assert blocks as likely inlined HSD JObj helper calls.

key_files.why: Largest churn (+4102/-4094) and largest reported data improvement: `main/melee/mp/mplib` `.data` +3040 bytes from 60.80% to 94.04%; `mpGetSpeed` reached 100%. Diff shows large static data/function ordering changes around `mpLib_803BF248`, `mpLib_803BF718`, `mpLib_803BF728`, `mpLib_803BF738`, and `mpLib_803BF760`.

Major game-mode matching win: `fn_80175DC8` +2751 bytes from 8.23% to 93.22% and `fn_80174B4C` +940 bytes from 10.70% to 91.99%. Current diff models `lbl_803B7B18` as a typed struct with `lookup` and `Vec3 translate`, replacing the reviewed `M2C_FIELD` style.

`fn_80161154` improved +339 bytes. Review flagged pragma use and repeated per-player blocks; current diff shows the repeated four-player standing/tie-break logic rewritten into `for (i = 0; i < 4; i++)` loops over `MatchPlayerData`.

Stage work with new `.rodata` and `.sdata` exact matches plus large improvements in `grKongo_801D7134` (+1408) and `grKongo_801D6AFC`. Diff adds typed table fields for `grKg_803E188C`, a typed hitbox constant `grKg_803B7FB0`, and loop cleanup after review noted repeated code.

`grVenom_80205F30` improved +769 bytes. Review explained that `jobj.h` assert/matrix-dirty blocks usually mean an inlined/run JObj function; current diff replaces direct rotate/translate mutation with `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationY`, and `HSD_JObjSetTranslate`.

`ftCo_8009F834` improved +485 bytes. Diff replaces scalar x/y/z stack temporaries with `Vec3` stack objects, removes `M2C_BITWISE`, and simplifies multiple `efAsync_Spawn` calls by dropping generated-looking extra argument plumbing.

Multiple common fighter ground/platform functions improved, including reported gains for `ftCo_800A4038`, `ftCo_800A6700`, and `ftCo_800A7AAC`. Diff expands stage/item/island checks and introduces semantic locals like `island_pos`, but still contains `#pragma dont_inline` in the slice.
Contributed multiple new exact matches: `fn_802FDA78` +355 bytes to 100%, `un_802FE260` +21 bytes to 100%, and `.sdata` to 100%.
Reviewer called out a likely fake match: replacing `HSD_JObjSetRotation(jobj, &euler_quat)` with manual `jobj.h` assert plus direct `jobj->rotate` assignment should be undone.

what_changed: Touched 57 `src/melee` files with +8034/-7234 lines in the current slice; no current changed-file entries for the reviewed `tools/changes_fmt.py` or `tools/project.py` paths.
PR validation stated: clean PR worktree at `ff551b3`, baseline from `dae3629`, `python3 configure.py --require-protos`, and `ninja changes_all`.

Saved-baseline report: matched code 70.39% (+9176 bytes), matched data 41.33% (+712 bytes), 13 new matches, 0 broken matches, 123 improvements in unmatched items, and 0 regressions in unmatched items.

New exact matches included `if_2FC93` `fn_802FDA78`, `ftafterimage` `.data`, `grkinokoroute` `.data`, `grkongo` `.rodata` and `.sdata`, `if_2FC93` `un_802FE260`, `ftchangeparam` `ftCo_800CF6E8`, `ifstock` `.sdata2`, `mpGetSpeed`, `gmstaffroll` `fn_801AA854`, and `grcorneria` `grCorneria_801DD534`.

Refactored generated-looking C patterns: scalar coordinate temporaries to `Vec3`, repeated four-slot player logic to loops, hand-expanded JObj assert/matrix-dirty code to HSD JObj setter calls, and byte-table/M2C access to typed structs.

Effect code in `efasync.c` and `efsync.c` adds intermediate float locals before `HSD_JObjSetRotationY` and adjusts queue access/casts, apparently for register/stack scheduling in effect spawn dispatch paths.

PR body said it added a strict local regression gate so `changes`/`changes_all` fail on backward movement; review comments requested reverting the `tools/changes_fmt.py` and `tools/project.py` changes, so this should be treated as contested unless confirmed in a later commit.
smart_moves: Using a saved baseline and publishing exact code/data deltas made the matching impact auditable and caught regressions early.

The current `gmresult.c` approach of modeling `lbl_803B7B18` as `struct { u8 lookup[34][2]; Vec3 translate; }` is a reusable way to eliminate `M2C_FIELD` and preserve the mixed lookup/vector data layout.
Review-guided recognition of `jobj.h` assert blocks as inlined JObj helper calls led to cleaner `HSD_JObjSetRotationY/Z` and `HSD_JObjSetTranslate` usage in `grvenom.c`.
Converting repeated per-player blocks in `gm_1601.c` into loops over `player_standings[i]` both addressed review feedback and made the intended four-player algorithm clearer.
Replacing fake scalar triples with `Vec3` stack temporaries in fighter/common code is a good matching tactic when the assembly is operating on vector-like objects.
Reordering and typing large static data tables in `mplib.c` targeted `.data` matching directly, not just function text, and produced the largest reported data improvement.
decomp_lessons: Reviewer guidance: repeated code is often an unrolled `for` loop; check for loop structure before preserving repeated m2c-style blocks.

Reviewer guidance: pointer math such as `(u8*)`, `base + offset`, and `base + offset + index * index_offset` is a red flag; prefer typed structs, arrays, `obj->field`, and `obj->field_arr[i]` when evidence supports it.
Reviewer guidance: pragmas are usually not how the original code was written; avoid `#pragma` matching shortcuts when a structural rewrite can match.
Reviewer guidance: `jobj.h` asserts generally indicate an inlined/run JObj helper, not source code that manually asserts and writes JObj fields.
Avoid fake matches: the `lb_00F9.c` review explicitly said direct `jobj->rotate` assignment in place of `HSD_JObjSetRotation` was likely a regression/fake match.
`M2C_FIELD` and `M2C_BITWISE` are cleanup targets; solving the underlying type/table shape can improve both match and maintainability.
Use local regression reports as a safety net, but keep tooling-policy changes separate or clearly agreed upon; this PR's regression-gate tool edits were explicitly requested for revert.
When a review comment is on an older hunk, check the current diff before recording it as unresolved: this slice shows some reviewed issues already addressed in later commits.
naming_conventions: No broad symbol-renaming theme is evidenced; unknown functions retain address names like `fn_80175DC8`, `fn_80161154`, `fn_802FDA78`, and `un_802FE260`.
Known functions keep module prefixes: `ftCo_`, `grKongo_`, `grVenom_`, `ifStock_`, `mnDiagram_`, `mpLib_`, and `mpGetSpeed`.
HSD and common game types keep canonical capitalization: `HSD_GObj`, `HSD_JObj`, `HSD_Text`, `Vec3`, `Point3d`, `MatchEnd`, and `MatchPlayerData`.

Static data labels such as `lbl_803B7B18`, `grKg_803B7FB0`, `grKg_803E188C`, and `mpLib_803BF248` are retained, but the diff adds typed fields like `lookup`, `translate`, `damage`, `kb_angle`, and `element` where understood.
Stack temporaries still use match-driven `spXX` names where necessary, but semantic locals like `player`, `pdata`, `island_pos`, `angle`, and `kept_gobj` are introduced when the role is clear.
Stage IDs appear as all-caps enum constants such as `KONGO`, `VENOM`, `PURA`, `STORY`, `ZEBES`, `ONETT`, `FIGURE1`, `FIGURE2`, and `FIGURE3`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `PAD_STACK` only as an evidence-backed stack-shape tool; this diff uses it widely, but review feedback discourages pragma-style shortcuts.
Introduce intermediate float locals when needed for register scheduling, as seen with `rot_y` to `f32_1` in `efasync.c` and `half_pi` to `va_f32_1` in `efsync.c`; reuse only with assembly proof.
Turn byte blobs plus `M2C_FIELD` into typed aggregate layouts when offsets reveal mixed data, as with `lbl_803B7B18.lookup` plus `lbl_803B7B18.translate`.

For HSD JObj code, prefer original helper calls (`HSD_JObjSetRotationY`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslate`, `HSD_JObjSetRotation`) over manually reproducing assert and dirty-matrix logic.
For repeated slot/table code, test loop forms over fixed counts like 4 players or 0xF table entries before accepting unrolled m2c output.
Data-section matching can depend on C definition order and linkage; `mplib.c` demonstrates large `.data` gains from moving/typing static table definitions and using extern declarations where needed.
Correct element sizes and signedness matter: `grKongo.c` changes `_struct_grKg_803E188C_0x18` from a single `s32` head field to `s16 unk0` plus `s16 unk2`, matching later indexed logic.

Removing generated-looking vararg extras from `efAsync_Spawn` sites can improve C shape, but because the callee is variadic this should be checked against the specific effect/gfx-id calling convention.

review_feedback.summary: General guidance: repeated code is often an unrolled for-loop; pointer math should become struct fields/indices; pragmas are usually not ideal; `jobj.h` asserts usually come from inlined JObj code.

review_feedback.current_slice_note: The current diff no longer shows the reviewed `#pragma push` / `#pragma global_optimizer off` hunk for this function.

review_feedback.comment: `fn_80161154` repeated code is most likely a for loop.
review_feedback.current_slice_note: Current diff uses `for (i = 0; i < 4; i++)` loops over `arg0->player_standings[i]`.

review_feedback.comment: Repeated `lb_80011E24`/slot setup may be a for loop; out-of-order 6/7 should be checked.
review_feedback.current_slice_note: Current diff still uses a loop over players but contains many switch-per-slot assignments; worth rechecking.
Current diff replaces the byte table with a typed `lbl_803B7B18` struct and uses `.translate`/`.lookup`.
Current diff includes an explicit `for (i = 0; i < 0xF; i++)` for the reviewed accumulation pattern.

review_feedback.comment: `jobj.h` asserts are indications of an included/run `jobj.c` function.
review_feedback.current_slice_note: Current diff uses HSD JObj setter calls for rotation and translation in several places.

review_feedback.comment: Undo direct `jobj->rotate` write; reviewer called it likely a regression/fake match and said `HSD_JObj` call was likely how it was written.
review_feedback.current_slice_note: Needs confirmation in latest head because this was a concrete fake-match warning.
Current changed-files list and diff slice do not include these tool files, but review comments remain.

follow_up_queries: Check the latest PR head after `d27639465fa82eb0802f54137ca68690a453d776` for a new review or merge state; this slice is still open with changes requested.
Search touched files for remaining `#pragma`, especially `#pragma dont_inline` in `ftCo_0A01.c`, and justify or remove them.
Search for remaining `M2C_FIELD`, `M2C_BITWISE`, `(u8*)`, and `base + offset` patterns in the touched source files.
Verify `lb_00F9.c` keeps or restores `HSD_JObjSetRotation` rather than a direct `jobj->rotate` fake match.
Confirm the reviewed `tools/changes_fmt.py` and `tools/project.py` regression-gate edits were reverted or deliberately moved to a separate accepted PR.
Run `python3 configure.py --require-protos` and `ninja changes_all` on the latest head and compare against the saved-baseline report for zero regressions.
